author: system
body: Epic created from PLAN.md sections on API enhancements. Covers analytics, webhooks, audit logging, bulk ops, rate limiting, API versioning, and report generation. This epic has the most downstream beads.

author: system
body: Epic created from PLAN.md frontend roadmap. Dashboard redesign is highest priority — it's the first thing users see. PWA and accessibility are important for mobile users.

author: system
body: Schema epic is foundational — many API and frontend beads depend on schemas being defined first. Pagination (br-011) is the critical-path bead here.

author: system
body: CRITICAL PATH: This is the highest-leverage bead in the graph. 12+ downstream beads depend on pagination schemas. PageRank: 0.92, Betweenness: 0.85. Implement this first to unblock the most work.

author: system
body: Design decision: Support both cursor-based and offset-based pagination. Cursor is more efficient for large datasets; offset is simpler for UI components. Let the consumer choose.

author: system
body: Bulk schemas enable mass operations. Max 500 items is a balance between usability and server load. Per-item error reporting is essential for partial failure handling.

author: system
body: Webhook schemas define the event contract. HMAC-SHA256 signing prevents unauthorized webhook consumption. Discriminated union ensures type-safe event payloads.

author: system
body: Analytics is the most user-visible improvement. MongoDB aggregation pipelines are powerful for this. Watch out for performance — add proper compound indexes on (userId, date, category) for the aggregation stages.

author: system
body: Retrofitting pagination is a wide-reaching change touching every list endpoint. Do this carefully — backward compatibility is essential. Default to page 1, limit 20 so existing clients don't break.

author: system
body: Audit logging is fire-and-forget — it must never block the main request. Consider using Mongoose middleware (post hooks) to capture before/after state automatically.

author: system
body: Webhook delivery must be resilient. Exponential backoff: 1s, 4s, 16s. Log every delivery attempt. Dead-letter queue for permanently failed deliveries.

author: system
body: WebSocket is the foundation for real-time features. JWT auth in the handshake is critical — never expose cross-user data. Consider using Socket.io for automatic reconnection handling.

author: system
body: Health endpoints are P0 for Kubernetes deployments. The liveness probe should be fast and always succeed if the process is alive. Readiness probe checks actual dependencies.

author: system
body: Dashboard redesign is the highest-impact frontend bead. KPI cards give users an instant overview of their financial health. Skeleton loading prevents layout shift.

author: system
body: Infinite scroll with TanStack Query's useInfiniteQuery is the way to go. Don't forget to handle the case where the user scrolls back up — keep previous pages in cache.

author: system
body: Analytics page is the second most important frontend bead after the dashboard. Recharts works well with shadcn/ui styling. Use the same HSL color system for chart colors.

author: system
body: Accessibility is a legal and ethical requirement. Start with automated axe-core checks, then do manual keyboard testing. VoiceOver testing is important for screen reader users.

author: system
body: Conversation persistence is foundational for the AI experience. Without it, users lose context every time the server restarts. TTL cleanup prevents unbounded storage growth.

author: system
body: This bead connects the context-engineering and agentic-ai packages. The knowledge graph provides financial rules and patterns that make AI responses more accurate and contextual.

author: system
body: CI pipeline is infrastructure bedrock. Turbo remote caching dramatically speeds up CI — cached builds can skip unchanged packages entirely. MongoDB service container is needed for API tests.

author: system
body: Security headers are P0 because they're zero-effort defense against common attacks. Helmet.js handles most of it. CSP needs careful configuration to not break legitimate functionality.

author: system
body: E2E tests are the ultimate confidence builder. They test the entire stack together. Playwright is fast, reliable, and has great CI integration. Keep tests focused on critical user journeys, not edge cases.

author: system
body: Security tests should be run on every CI pipeline. NoSQL injection is particularly relevant for MongoDB — test for $gt, $ne, $regex operator injection in query parameters and request bodies.
